evals.prompt: Is LDL cholesterol actually a causal risk factor for coronary heart disease, or is the link just an observational correlation? Give me the Mendelian randomization evidence with effect direction and instrument quality.
evals.expected_output: A causal verdict (YES, causal) supported by an MR estimate with beta>0 / OR>1, high MOE, and a reverse-causation (bidirectional MR) check showing no meaningful CHD->LDL effect.
assertions: Resolves trait labels via EpiGraphDB_search_opengwas before the MR call
Calls EpiGraphDB_get_mendelian_randomization with exposure 'LDL cholesterol' and outcome 'Coronary heart disease'
Reports a positive causal effect (beta>0 / OR>1) and an OR per SD interpretation
Performs bidirectional MR (CHD -> LDL) to check reverse causation
States instrument-quality (MOE) and at least one assumption/pleiotropy caveat

evals.prompt: Does BMI cause type 2 diabetes, or do they just share genetics? I want a causal answer with the effect size and the main caveats.
evals.expected_output: A causal verdict (YES, causal, large effect) from MR with beta>0, distinguished explicitly from genetic correlation, plus a reverse-causation check and ancestry/pleiotropy caveats.
assertions: Calls EpiGraphDB_get_mendelian_randomization for Body mass index -> Type 2 diabetes
Reports a positive, large causal effect with an odds-ratio interpretation
Explicitly distinguishes causal MR evidence from (non-causal) genetic correlation
Checks reverse causation with bidirectional MR (T2D -> BMI)
Notes European-ancestry instruments and pleiotropy as limitations

evals.prompt: is high crp causally linked to stroke risk? use mendelian randomization
evals.expected_output: Resolves CRP and stroke to exact OpenGWAS trait labels first, runs MR, and interprets the result honestly — including the case where the exact-label MR returns empty (reads metadata.note and retries / reports inconclusive rather than asserting 'no effect').
assertions: Resolves 'CRP' / 'C-reactive protein' and 'stroke' to exact trait labels via EpiGraphDB_search_opengwas
Does NOT report an empty MR result as 'no causal effect' — reads metadata.note and treats a label miss as inconclusive
Gives a calibrated verdict (supported / not supported / inconclusive) with caveats rather than overclaiming
